projects.title: Hello, World! with Comments
projects.description: Write a basic C program that prints 'Hello, World!' and includes single-line and multi-line comments explaining each part.
projects.learningOutcome: Understanding basic program structure, headers, main function, return value, and comments.
projects.logicExplanation: A basic program to print a message, demonstrating the structure of a C program.
projects.codeExplanation: Includes stdio.h, defines main(), uses printf to output text, and returns 0.

projects.title: Variables and Format Specifiers
projects.description: Declare variables of types int, float, double, and char. Assign values and print them using appropriate format specifiers.
projects.learningOutcome: Variable declaration, initialization, and using printf format specifiers (%d, %f, %lf, %c).
projects.logicExplanation: Declare variables of different types and print them using appropriate format specifiers.
projects.codeExplanation: int, float, double, char are declared, assigned values, and printed with %d, %f, %lf, %c.

projects.title: Constants with const and #define
projects.description: Define PI as a constant using both const keyword and #define preprocessor. Compute the area and circumference of a circle for a given radius.
projects.exampleOutput:
Area (using const): 78.539816
Circumference (using const): 31.415926
Area (using #define): 78.539816
Circumference (using #define): 31.415926
projects.learningOutcome: Using const and #define to create constants, basic arithmetic.
projects.logicExplanation: Use both `const` and `#define` to define PI and compute area/circumference of a circle.
projects.codeExplanation: PI defined as const double and via #define. Program reads radius, calculates area and circumference, prints results.

projects.title: Arithmetic Operators Demonstration
projects.description: Read two integers and perform all arithmetic operations: addition, subtraction, multiplication, division (integer), and modulus. Print the results.
projects.logicExplanation: Perform all basic arithmetic operations (+, -, *, /, %) on two integers.
projects.codeExplanation: Read two ints, compute and print sum, difference, product, quotient, remainder.

projects.title: Relational Operators – Compare Two Numbers
projects.description: Read two integers and compare them using all relational operators (>, <, >=, <=, ==, !=). Print 1 if true, 0 if false.
projects.learningOutcome: Understanding relational operators and their integer results.
projects.logicExplanation: Compare two numbers using all relational operators and print the results (1 for true, 0 for false).
projects.codeExplanation: Read two ints, print outcomes of >, <, >=, <=, ==, !=.

projects.title: Logical Operators – Leap Year Check
projects.description: Check if a given year is a leap year using logical operators (&&, ||). A year is a leap year if divisible by 400 or (divisible by 4 and not by 100).
projects.learningOutcome: Combining conditions with logical AND and OR.
projects.logicExplanation: Check leap year using logical && and ||: (year%400==0) or (year%4==0 and year%100!=0).
projects.codeExplanation: Function isLeap() implements condition; main reads year and prints result.

projects.title: Assignment and Compound Assignment Operators
projects.description: Demonstrate simple assignment and compound operators +=, -=, *=, /=, %=. Start with an initial value, apply each operator with a user-provided number, and print results.
projects.logicExplanation: Standard algorithm for Assignment and Compound Assignment Operators.
projects.codeExplanation: The C code implements Assignment and Compound Assignment Operators as described.

projects.title: Increment and Decrement Operators (Prefix vs Postfix)
projects.description: Show the difference between prefix (++i) and postfix (i++) increment/decrement operators.
projects.learningOutcome: Prefix and postfix operators and their side effects.
projects.logicExplanation: Show difference between prefix (++i) and postfix (i++) increment/decrement.
projects.codeExplanation: Declare a variable, use it in expressions with prefix/postfix, print values to illustrate order.

projects.title: Type Conversion – Implicit and Explicit
projects.description: Demonstrate implicit conversion (e.g., int to float in division) and explicit casting (e.g., (float)int).
projects.exampleText:
Enter an integer: 7
Enter a float: 3.14
projects.exampleOutput:
Implicit: int / int stored in float: 7/2 = 3.000000
Explicit cast: (float)7/2 = 3.500000
Casting float to int: (int)3.14 = 3
projects.learningOutcome: Implicit type conversion, explicit casting, type promotion.
projects.logicExplanation: Demonstrate implicit conversion (int to float) and explicit casting.
projects.codeExplanation: Show integer division stored in float vs. explicit cast; also cast float to int (truncation).

projects.title: Operator Precedence – Evaluate Complex Expression
projects.description: Evaluate an expression involving multiple operators (+, -, *, /) to understand precedence and associativity.
projects.exampleText: Enter a, b, c, d, e: 2 3 4 5 6
projects.logicExplanation: Evaluate a complex expression with multiple operators to understand precedence and associativity.
projects.codeExplanation: Compute an expression like a + b * c - d / e and show step-by-step evaluation.

projects.title: Formatted Input with scanf – Read Multiple Types
projects.description: Use scanf to read an integer, a float, and a character, then print them back using printf with appropriate format specifiers.
projects.exampleText:
Enter an integer: 10
Enter a float: 3.14
Enter a character: A
projects.exampleOutput: You entered: integer = 10, float = 3.140000, character = A
projects.learningOutcome: Using format specifiers with scanf for different data types.
projects.logicExplanation: Use scanf to read different data types (int, float, char) with proper format specifiers.
projects.codeExplanation: Read an integer, a float, and a character; print them back using printf.

projects.title: While Loop – Print Numbers 1 to N
projects.description: Print numbers from 1 to N using a while loop.
projects.logicExplanation: Use a while loop to print numbers from 1 to N.
projects.codeExplanation: Initialize counter, loop while <= N, print and increment.

projects.title: While Loop – Sum of First N Natural Numbers
projects.description: Compute the sum of first N natural numbers using a while loop.
projects.logicExplanation: Standard algorithm for While Loop – Sum of First N Natural Numbers.
projects.codeExplanation: The C code implements While Loop – Sum of First N Natural Numbers as described.

projects.title: While Loop – Product (Factorial) of N
projects.description: Compute the factorial of a non-negative integer using a while loop.
projects.logicExplanation: Compute factorial of N using a while loop (iterative).
projects.codeExplanation: Initialize product=1, multiply by i from 1 to N.

projects.title: Do-While Loop – Menu-Driven Calculator
projects.description: Create a menu-driven calculator that repeats until user chooses exit, using a do-while loop.
projects.exampleText:
1. Add
2. Subtract
3. Multiply
4. Divide
5. Exit
Enter choice: 1
Enter two numbers: 10 5
Result = 15
projects.learningOutcome: Do-while loop for menus, switch statement.
projects.logicExplanation: Present a menu of operations and repeat until user chooses exit using do-while.
projects.codeExplanation: Display menu, read choice, perform operation with switch, loop while choice != exit.

projects.title: Do-While Loop – Guess the Number Game
projects.description: Generate a random number between 1 and 100, and let the user guess it with hints, using do-while.
projects.exampleText:
Guess the number (1-100): 50
Too low!
Guess again: 75
Too high!
...
projects.exampleOutput: Congratulations! You guessed it in X tries.
projects.learningOutcome: Random numbers, do-while for at least one iteration.
projects.logicExplanation: Generate random number, let user guess with hints, repeat until correct using do-while.
projects.codeExplanation: Use rand() and srand(), do-while to keep asking, give feedback.

projects.title: For Loop – Multiplication Table
projects.description: Print the multiplication table of a given number using a for loop.
projects.logicExplanation: Print multiplication table of a number using for loop.
projects.codeExplanation: Loop from 1 to 10, print number * i.

projects.title: For Loop – Sum of Squares
projects.description: Compute the sum of squares: 1² + 2² + ... + n² using a for loop.
projects.logicExplanation: Compute sum of squares 1² + 2² + ... + n² using for loop.
projects.codeExplanation: Accumulate i*i in loop.

projects.title: For Loop – Factorial (Iterative)
projects.description: Compute factorial of a number using a for loop.
projects.logicExplanation: Compute factorial using for loop.
projects.codeExplanation: Initialize fact=1, for i=1 to n, fact *= i.

projects.title: For Loop – Fibonacci Series
projects.description: Generate the Fibonacci series up to N terms using a for loop.
projects.logicExplanation: Generate Fibonacci series up to N terms using for loop.
projects.codeExplanation: Initialize a=0, b=1; for loop to print and update.

projects.title: Break in Loop – Find First Number Divisible by 5 and 7
projects.description: Find the first number in a given range that is divisible by both 5 and 7 using break.
projects.exampleOutput: First number divisible by 5 and 7 is 35
projects.learningOutcome: Using break to exit loop early.
projects.logicExplanation: Find first number divisible by both 5 and 7 in a range using break.
projects.codeExplanation: Loop from start to end, if condition true, print and break.

projects.title: Continue in Loop – Skip Multiples of 3
projects.description: Print numbers from 1 to 20, skipping multiples of 3 using continue.
projects.logicExplanation: Print numbers 1-20 skipping multiples of 3 using continue.
projects.codeExplanation: Loop 1 to 20, if i%3==0 continue, else print.

projects.title: Prime Number Check
projects.description: Check whether a given number is prime using a loop up to sqrt(n).
projects.logicExplanation: Check if a number is prime using loop up to sqrt(n).
projects.codeExplanation: Handle <2, loop from 2 to sqrt(n), if divisible return 0; else 1.

projects.title: Prime Numbers in a Given Range
projects.description: Print all prime numbers between two given integers using a function to check primality.
projects.logicExplanation: Standard algorithm for Prime Numbers in a Given Range.
projects.codeExplanation: The C code implements Prime Numbers in a Given Range as described.

projects.title: GCD (Euclidean Algorithm)
projects.description: Compute the greatest common divisor of two integers using the Euclidean algorithm (loop version).
projects.logicExplanation: Compute GCD using Euclidean algorithm (loop).
projects.codeExplanation: While b != 0, set temp=b, b=a%b, a=temp; return a.

projects.title: LCM Using GCD
projects.description: Compute the least common multiple of two integers using the formula LCM = a * b / GCD(a,b).
projects.logicExplanation: Compute GCD using Euclidean algorithm (loop).
projects.codeExplanation: While b != 0, set temp=b, b=a%b, a=temp; return a.

projects.title: Reverse a Number
projects.description: Reverse the digits of a given integer using a loop.
projects.learningOutcome: Digit extraction and building reversed number.
projects.logicExplanation: Reverse the digits of an integer using loop.
projects.codeExplanation: While n!=0, rev = rev*10 + n%10, n/=10.

projects.title: Palindrome Number
projects.description: Check if a number is a palindrome (reads the same forwards and backwards).
projects.logicExplanation: Check if a number is palindrome by comparing with its reverse.
projects.codeExplanation: Call reverseNumber() and compare with original.

projects.title: Sum of Digits
projects.description: Compute the sum of digits of a positive integer.
projects.logicExplanation: Sum digits of a number using loop.
projects.codeExplanation: While n!=0, sum += n%10, n/=10.

projects.title: Armstrong Number
projects.description: Check if a number is an Armstrong number (sum of its digits raised to the power of number of digits equals the number).
projects.logicExplanation: Check if number equals sum of its digits raised to power of number of digits.
projects.codeExplanation: Count digits, compute sum of pow(d, digits), compare.

projects.title: Perfect Number
projects.description: Check if a number is perfect (equal to sum of its proper divisors).
projects.logicExplanation: Check if number equals sum of its proper divisors.
projects.codeExplanation: Loop from 1 to n/2, sum divisors, compare.

projects.title: Count Digits in a Number
projects.description: Count the number of digits in a positive integer.
projects.logicExplanation: Count number of digits in an integer using loop.
projects.codeExplanation: Handle 0 case, while n!=0, count++, n/=10.

projects.title: Power of a Number (Iterative)
projects.description: Compute base raised to exponent (non-negative) using iterative multiplication.
projects.logicExplanation: Standard algorithm for Power of a Number (Iterative).
projects.codeExplanation: The C code implements Power of a Number (Iterative) as described.

projects.title: Power of a Number (Recursive)
projects.description: Compute base raised to exponent using recursion.
projects.logicExplanation: Standard algorithm for Power of a Number (Recursive).
projects.codeExplanation: The C code implements Power of a Number (Recursive) as described.

projects.title: Fibonacci Number (Recursive)
projects.description: Compute the nth Fibonacci number using recursion.
projects.logicExplanation: Standard algorithm for Fibonacci Number (Recursive).
projects.codeExplanation: The C code implements Fibonacci Number (Recursive) as described.

projects.title: Factorial (Recursive)
projects.description: Compute factorial of a number using recursion.
projects.logicExplanation: Compute factorial recursively.
projects.codeExplanation: Base case n<=1 return 1; else return n * factorialRec(n-1).

projects.title: Nested Loops – Right-Angled Triangle Pattern
projects.description: Print a right-angled triangle of stars using nested loops.
projects.logicExplanation: Print right-angled triangle of stars using nested loops.
projects.codeExplanation: Outer loop rows, inner loop prints stars equal to row number.

projects.title: Nested Loops – Inverted Right-Angled Triangle
projects.description: Print an inverted right-angled triangle of stars.
projects.logicExplanation: Print right-angled triangle of stars using nested loops.
projects.codeExplanation: Outer loop rows, inner loop prints stars equal to row number.

projects.title: Nested Loops – Pyramid Pattern
projects.description: Print a centered pyramid of stars.
projects.learningOutcome: Nested loops with spaces and stars.
projects.logicExplanation: Print centered pyramid of stars.
projects.codeExplanation: Outer loop rows, inner loops for spaces and stars.

projects.title: Nested Loops – Diamond Pattern
projects.description: Print a diamond pattern of stars.
projects.logicExplanation: Print diamond pattern using nested loops.
projects.codeExplanation: Upper half pyramid, lower half inverted pyramid.

projects.title: Nested Loops – Number Pattern (1, 12, 123...)
projects.description: Print a pattern where each row contains numbers from 1 to row number.
projects.logicExplanation: Print pattern: 1, 12, 123, ... using nested loops.
projects.codeExplanation: Outer loop rows, inner loop prints numbers from 1 to row.

projects.title: Nested Loops – Floyd's Triangle
projects.description: Print Floyd's triangle: consecutive numbers arranged in rows.
projects.learningOutcome: Using a counter in nested loops.
projects.logicExplanation: Print Floyd's triangle (consecutive numbers).
projects.codeExplanation: Use counter, nested loops to print numbers in triangular form.

projects.title: Nested Loops – Multiplication Table Grid
projects.description: Print a 10x10 multiplication table grid using nested loops.
projects.logicExplanation: Print 10x10 multiplication table grid using nested loops.
projects.codeExplanation: Outer loop 1-10, inner loop 1-10, print product formatted.

projects.title: Debugging Loops – Infinite Loop Example
projects.description: Demonstrate an infinite loop (commented out) and show how to fix it.
projects.logicExplanation: Demonstrate an infinite loop (commented out) and show how to fix.
projects.codeExplanation: Show loop with condition always true, then corrected version.

projects.title: Loop Boundary Errors – Off-by-One Demonstration
projects.description: Illustrate off-by-one errors in loops and show corrected versions.
projects.logicExplanation: Illustrate off-by-one errors in loops and correct them.
projects.codeExplanation: Show loops that miss first/last element and corrected versions.

projects.title: Using Constants for Loop Limits
projects.description: Use #define to define a constant for the loop limit and print numbers 1 to that constant.
projects.logicExplanation: Use #defined constants for loop limits to improve readability.
projects.codeExplanation: Define MAX=10, loop from 1 to MAX, print values.

projects.title: Sum of Even Numbers from 1 to N
projects.description: Compute the sum of all even numbers between 1 and N using a loop.
projects.logicExplanation: Compute sum of even numbers from 1 to N using loop.
projects.codeExplanation: Loop 1 to N, if i%2==0 add to sum.

projects.title: Sum of Odd Numbers from 1 to N
projects.description: Compute the sum of all odd numbers between 1 and N.
projects.logicExplanation: Compute sum of odd numbers from 1 to N using loop.
projects.codeExplanation: Loop 1 to N, if i%2!=0 add to sum.

projects.title: Even or Odd Using Conditional Operator
projects.description: Check if a number is even or odd using the conditional (ternary) operator.
projects.logicExplanation: Check if a number is even or odd using conditional operator.
projects.codeExplanation: Read number, use (n%2==0) ? 'Even' : 'Odd' and print.

projects.title: Simple Interest Calculation
projects.description: Calculate simple interest given principal, rate, and time using formula SI = (P * R * T) / 100.
projects.logicExplanation: Calculate simple interest: SI = (P * R * T) / 100.
projects.codeExplanation: Read principal, rate, time; compute and print interest.
